# Brain Memory

> A hierarchical, file-system-based memory system for AI coding agents, inspired by human neuroscience — memories decay on an Ebbinghaus curve, strengthen through recall, connect via associative networks, and consolidate during a sleep cycle. A deterministic recall engine gives identical scoring across Claude Code, Gemini CLI, OpenAI Codex CLI, and OpenCode: one brain, any model, every agent.

Brain Memory stores every memory as a human-readable Markdown file with YAML frontmatter in a single global `~/.brain/` directory — no database, no server. It is free and open source (npm package: `brain-memory`).

## Getting Started

- [Overview](https://brainmemory.work/docs): Introduction to Brain Memory — a neuroscience-inspired memory system for AI coding agents.
- [Installation](https://brainmemory.work/docs/getting-started/installation): Install the Brain Memory plugin for Claude Code, Gemini CLI, or OpenAI Codex.
- [Quick Start](https://brainmemory.work/docs/getting-started/quick-start): Get up and running with Brain Memory in under 5 minutes.

## Concepts

- [Memory Types](https://brainmemory.work/docs/concepts/memory-types): Learn about the 8 memory types: decision, insight, goal, experience, learning, relationship, preference, and observation.
- [Cognitive Types](https://brainmemory.work/docs/concepts/cognitive-types): Understand episodic, semantic, and procedural memory — modeled after human cognition.
- [Strength & Decay](https://brainmemory.work/docs/concepts/strength-decay): How memories strengthen through recall and weaken over time via exponential decay.
- [Associative Network](https://brainmemory.work/docs/concepts/associative-network): Memories connect via weighted edges with spreading activation and Hebbian learning.
- [Context-Dependent Recall](https://brainmemory.work/docs/concepts/context-recall): Memories encoded in a similar context to the current session are scored higher during recall.
- [Salience & Confidence](https://brainmemory.work/docs/concepts/salience-confidence): Emotional significance and epistemic certainty scores that influence memory behavior.
- [Pinned Memory](https://brainmemory.work/docs/concepts/pinned-memory): Pin critical conventions and preferences to the always-present tier — loaded every session, never decaying.
- [Procedural Skills](https://brainmemory.work/docs/concepts/procedural-skills): Reusable how-to workflows served via progressive disclosure and learned from repeated experience.

## Commands

- [init](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/init): Initialize the ~/.brain/ directory structure and default configuration.
- [memorize](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/memorize): Store new memories from the current session context with automatic categorization.
- [remember](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/remember): Recall relevant memories using spreading activation and context-dependent scoring.
- [pin](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/pin): Pin a memory to the always-present tier — loaded every session, never decaying.
- [unpin](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/unpin): Remove a memory from the always-present tier, returning it to normal recall and decay.
- [review](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/review): Spaced repetition review session for memories due for reinforcement.
- [explore](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/explore): Browse the brain hierarchy and discover memories by category.
- [consolidate](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/consolidate): Merge related weak memories into stronger combined memories.
- [forget](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/forget): Decay or archive memories that are no longer relevant.
- [sunshine](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/sunshine): Deep forensic erasure — trace and remove all references to a memory.
- [sleep](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/sleep): Full maintenance cycle: replay, synaptic homeostasis, consolidation, pruning, and REM dreaming.
- [status](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/status): Dashboard with brain health overview, memory counts, and strength distribution.
- [skill](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/skill): Manage procedural skills — reusable how-to workflows with progressive disclosure.
- [sync](https://brainmemory.work/docs/commands/sync): Sync memories via Git remote or export/import for cross-device portability.

## Advanced

- [Scoring Formula](https://brainmemory.work/docs/advanced/scoring-formula): The v4 recall scoring formula: relevance, decayed strength, recency, spreading bonus, context match, and salience.
- [Spaced Reinforcement](https://brainmemory.work/docs/advanced/spaced-reinforcement): How recall spacing affects strength boosts and decay rate improvements.
- [Sleep Cycle](https://brainmemory.work/docs/advanced/sleep-cycle): Deep dive into the sleep maintenance cycle: replay, homeostasis, crystallization, and dreaming.
- [Cross-Agent Sharing](https://brainmemory.work/docs/advanced/cross-agent): Share memories across Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and OpenAI Codex agents.
- [Portable Sync](https://brainmemory.work/docs/advanced/portable-sync): Git-based sync and encrypted export/import for cross-device memory portability.
- [Working Memory & Session Start](https://brainmemory.work/docs/advanced/working-memory): How Brain feeds the context window at session start through a single budget-bounded aggregator that never overflows working memory.

## Reference

- [File Structure](https://brainmemory.work/docs/reference/file-structure): Complete reference for the ~/.brain/ directory layout and file organization.
- [Memory File Format](https://brainmemory.work/docs/reference/memory-format): YAML frontmatter schema and Markdown body format for memory files.
- [Configuration](https://brainmemory.work/docs/reference/configuration): Configuration options for decay rates, scoring weights, and sync settings.
- [Changelog](https://brainmemory.work/docs/reference/changelog): Release history and version notes for the Brain Memory plugin.

## Benchmarks

- [Overview](https://brainmemory.work/docs/benchmarks): Controlled benchmark suite proving Brain Memory improves retrieval, contradiction handling, and token efficiency for AI coding agents.
- [Methodology](https://brainmemory.work/docs/benchmarks/methodology): How retrieval is scored, why the judge is cross-family, distractor haystacks, and references to the 2025-2026 SOTA the design follows.
- [Scenarios](https://brainmemory.work/docs/benchmarks/scenarios): The six benchmark scenarios — each describable in one sentence — with project fixtures, rubrics, and arm matrices.
- [Results](https://brainmemory.work/docs/benchmarks/results): Current measurements from the benchmark suite, with raw JSON downloads.

## Optional

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/omelas-tech/brain): source code and issues
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/brain-memory): install with `npm install -g brain-memory`
- [Full documentation corpus](https://brainmemory.work/llms-full.txt): every page concatenated as plain text
